summary: Gruber updates a previously posted AppleScript for opening multiple files together in a single new BBEdit text window. The original script required workarounds due to BBEdit 8.0's limited scripting support for its documents drawer. BBEdit 8.1 added an 'opening in' parameter to the open command, making the script much simpler. The new version is more concise, intuitive, and faster than the original. He also notes Chris Nandor's updated Perl version of a similar script.
key_points: BBEdit 8.1 added an 'opening in' parameter with options for front_window, separate_windows, and new_window, simplifying multi-file opening
The updated AppleScript is significantly shorter and cleaner than the BBEdit 8.0 workaround version
Gruber uses the script with Ranchero's Big Cat Scripts plug-in for Finder contextual menu integration
money_quotes: BBEdit 8.1 makes this a lot easier, by adding an optional opening in parameter to the open command.
This script is not only more concise and intuitive than the version from September, but executes a bit quicker as well.
key_insight: BBEdit 8.1's improved scripting support for the documents drawer eliminated the need for workarounds, turning a clunky multi-step AppleScript into an elegant one-liner.
